content_scan.note: Binary assets are counted by path only; asset internals require Unreal Asset Registry or editor-side analysis.

recommended_first_pass: Inventory reflected C++ API and Blueprint-exposed surface.
Classify native-heavy systems before attempting any BP graph authoring.
Generate BP shells or graph glue only for low/medium risk classes with existing native parent types.
Keep GAS, replication, GameFeatures, Slate/editor modules, and custom K2 as native-blocked until dedicated support exists.

supported_categories.label: Blueprint graph control flow and expressions
supported_categories.note: MCP can author common graph constructs, but arbitrary C++ expression lowering remains heuristic.

supported_categories.label: Actor and component composition
supported_categories.note: Blueprint shells and component setup are feasible when behavior is simple and component classes already exist.

supported_categories.label: Enhanced Input
supported_categories.note: Event node authoring exists; full asset/data migration and trigger/modifier semantics need separate handling.

supported_categories.label: Blueprint-exposed native API
supported_categories.note: Existing MCP tools can call exposed functions and author graph glue, but native implementations remain native.

supported_categories.label: Reflected Unreal API surface
supported_categories.note: Reflection macros can be inventoried and partially mapped to Blueprint shells, variables, and callable nodes.

partial_categories.label: CommonUI and UMG
partial_categories.note: UMG wrappers help, but CommonUI activation/layer policy and widget trees need dedicated analyzer support.

partial_categories.label: Gameplay Ability System
partial_categories.note: Blueprint children and glue may be feasible, but core ASC, attributes, prediction, and ability tasks remain native-heavy.

partial_categories.label: Delegates, async actions, and latent flow
partial_categories.note: Binding and callback topology are detectable, but robust delegate/event authoring needs more MCP graph primitives.

partial_categories.label: Subsystem and lifecycle orchestration
partial_categories.note: Lifecycle hooks can be called around, but authoritative subsystem behavior should remain native or get explicit support.

partial_categories.label: Gameplay Tags
partial_categories.note: Tags can be referenced through exposed APIs; tag table authoring and semantic migration are separate concerns.

partial_categories.label: Data assets and data registries
partial_categories.note: Asset classes can be discovered; data asset creation/editing needs editor-side asset tooling beyond graph authoring.

blocked_categories.label: Slate and editor-only C++
blocked_categories.note: Editor modules and Slate widgets should be classified out of runtime BP conversion scope.

blocked_categories.label: Networking and replication
blocked_categories.note: Replication policy, prediction, RPC authority, and replication graph behavior are not safe to auto-lower to BP.

blocked_categories.label: Game Features and Modular Gameplay
blocked_categories.note: Plugin activation, experience loading, and component injection are project architecture concerns, not just BP graph authoring.

blocked_categories.label: Custom K2 and native graph extensions
blocked_categories.note: Custom K2 node behavior is native editor extension code and cannot be recreated by generic BP authoring alone.
